channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials requested a court warrant on Monday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials plan to conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country’s airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a weekend plane crash that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Gaza's crowded tent camps, women wrestle with a life stripped of privacy
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza City (AP) — For Gaza’s women, the hardships of life in the territory’s sprawling tent camps are compounded by the daily humiliation of never having privacy.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eyewitnesses to South Korea plane crash recount sparks in engine, bird strike before collision
item.description: Witnesses to a deadly plane crash in South Korea said they observed flames in the engine of the aircraft, heard explosions and noticed birds colliding with the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2024 was a year of triumphs and setbacks for Russian leader Vladimir Putin. Here's how it unfolded
item.description: In 2024, President Vladimir Putin further cemented his grip on power and sought to counter Russia's isolation from the West over the war in Ukraine. But he faced continuing challenges, with a deadly attack by gunmen in Moscow and an incursion by Kyiv's troops on his territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel detains the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals during a raid
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's army detained the director of one of northern Gaza 's last functioning hospitals as overnight strikes elsewhere in the territory killed nine people, including children, Palestinian medical officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk penned an op-ed further expressing support for the far-right German political party Alternative for Germany (AfD). In response, the paper’s commentary editor resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Former Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, who instituted economic reforms, cremated in New Delhi
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Manmohan Singh, the former Indian prime minister widely regarded as the architect of the country’s economic reform program, was cremated after a state funeral on Saturday as politicians and the public mourned his death.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli airstrikes hit a Yemen airport as a jet with hundreds onboard was landing, UN official says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen’s main airport as a civilian Airbus 320 with hundreds of passengers on board was landing and a U.N. delegation was waiting to leave, the U.N.'s top humanitarian official in Yemen said Friday. Julien Harneis told U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO will step up Baltic Sea patrols as Finland probes the possible sabotage of undersea cables
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Friday that the military alliance will step up patrols in the Baltic Sea region as Finnish investigators work to establish whether a ship linked to Russia sabotaged undersea cables there this week.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines blames deadly plane crash on 'external interference' as Russia speculation grows
item.description: Azerbaijan Airlines has suspended flights to multiple Russian airports in the wake of a deadly plane crash, which it attributed to "physical and technical external interference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: President Yoon Suk Yeol off set political crisis in South Korea after imposing martial law. The country's National Assembly has voted to impeach President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Armed for survival: How Oct 7 Hamas massacre transformed gun culture in Israel
item.description: In the wake of the unprecedented October. 7 Hamas terrorist attacks, Israelis are having to grapple with the new reality of firearm ownership in a country where owning guns was once uncommon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia downplays speculation over deadly Azerbaijan Airlines crash as report lays blame for downed plane
item.description: Officials in Russia and Kazakhstan are urging people to wait for an investigation into the deadly Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash before assigning blame.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grandson of former commandant of Auschwitz on rise of antisemitism, his life as a pastor
item.description: Kai Höss, the grandson of former Auschwitz Commandant Rudolf Höss, speaks with Fox News Digital about antisemitism today and how he reconciles his Christian faith with his grandfather's past.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
